text.title: C# .NET Patterns Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to produce a clean-arch + CQRS feature folder.

step.name: verify-layer-graph
step.action: Confirm Domain → no deps; Application → Domain; Infrastructure → Application; Web → Application + Infrastructure. Add `<ProjectReference>` analyzers to enforce.
step.decision-gate: If Domain references EF or ASP.NET Core, FAIL and remove the reference first.

step.name: define-command-and-handler
step.action: Write `record ShipOrderCommand(int OrderId, ...) : IRequest<ShipOrderResponse>` in `Application/Orders/`. Write `ShipOrderHandler : IRequestHandler<ShipOrderCommand, ShipOrderResponse>` next to it. Constructor takes repository + unit of work.

step.name: aggregate-behaviour
step.action: Add intention-revealing method to the aggregate (`order.Ship(carrier, when)`). Method enforces invariants (status transitions, required fields) and raises a domain event. No public setters introduced.

step.name: validator-and-dto
step.action: Write `ShipOrderValidator : AbstractValidator<ShipOrderCommand>` with `RuleFor(...).NotEmpty().MaximumLength(...)`. Define `ShipOrderResponse` record. Register validator in DI via `AddValidatorsFromAssembly`.

step.name: controller-and-test
step.action: Web controller becomes one line: `await _mediator.Send(req, ct)`. Write xUnit handler test that mocks repository + asserts response. Reject the PR if controller has any business logic.
step.decision-gate: If the test asserts EF behaviour rather than handler logic, the handler is not isolated — refactor.
